raw_response: I agree with the chosen option B - Ureteric reimplantation. One additional point supporting this view is that while Teflon injections might seem appealing due to their non-invasive nature, they carry higher recurrence rates compared to ureteric reimplantation. Recurrence after Teflon injection ranges between 50% to 78%, whereas success rates for ureteric reimplantation typically exceed 90%. Given the severity and bilateral nature of the reflux, ensuring a durable outcome is crucial, making ureteric reimplantation the superior choice. Hence, my final answer remains B. Answer choice: B.
